01_initial_request.input_data: blaIMP-1 and blaIMP-6 gene sequences and protein variants (Ser262 vs Gly262) from clinical isolates
01_initial_request.user_intent: To investigate the structural and functional impact of the Ser262Gly mutation on substrate specificity and loop flexibility
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Michaelis constant (Km) values and substrate affinity metrics for imipenem and meropenem

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Clinical isolates Escherichia coli 58–132 and NR390 containing blaIMP-1 and blaIMP-6 genes are maintained at Nara Medical University Hospital. [Gap] The gene sequences need to be amplified for cloning into expression vectors. [Decision] To achieve gene amplification, chose to use PCR to perform amplification on blaIMP-1 and blaIMP-6 genes, expecting to obtain PCR products.

parameters.template: blaIMP-1 and blaIMP-6 genes from clinical isolates

02_agent_trajectory.observation: PCR products of blaIMP-1 and blaIMP-6 genes obtained.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PCR products and pET28a vector are available. [Gap] The DNA fragments need to be prepared for ligation. [Decision] To achieve vector and insert preparation, chose to use Restriction Enzymes to perform digestion on PCR products and vector, expecting to obtain digested fragments.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Digested vector and inserts ready for ligation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Digested vector and inserts are prepared. [Gap] The fragments need to be joined to form plasmids. [Decision] To achieve plasmid construction, chose to use T4 ligase to perform ligation on vector and inserts, expecting to obtain pET28a-imp6 and pET28a-imp1 plasmids.

[Background] Recombinant plasmids are constructed. [Gap] The plasmids need to be introduced into host cells for expression. [Decision] To achieve protein expression host preparation, chose to use Transformation to perform transformation on Escherichia coli BL21(DE3) cells, expecting to obtain transformants.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: E. coli transformants carrying blaIMP-6 or blaIMP-1 obtained.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Transformants are available. [Gap] The recombinant proteins need to be expressed. [Decision] To achieve protein production, chose to use Cell Culture to perform induction on E. coli transformants, expecting to obtain cell pellets containing IMP-6 and IMP-1.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cells expressing IMP-6 or IMP-1 harvested.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cell pellets containing recombinant enzymes are harvested. [Gap] The enzymes need to be purified from cell extract. [Decision] To achieve initial purification, chose to use Macro-Prep High S Support column to perform ion exchange chromatography on cell-free extract, expecting to obtain partially purified enzyme fractions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fractions containing IMP-6 or IMP-1 pooled.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Partially purified enzyme fractions are pooled. [Gap] Further purification is needed for crystallization. [Decision] To achieve high purity, chose to use CM-Toyopearl 650S column to perform ion exchange chromatography on pooled fractions, expecting to obtain purified IMP-6 and IMP-1.

parameters.buffer: Buffer A (20 mM Tris/HCl pH 7.5, 50 uM ZnSO4)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Purified IMP-6 and IMP-1 enzymes obtained.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Purified enzymes are available. [Gap] Crystals are needed for structure determination. [Decision] To achieve crystallization, chose to use Hanging-drop vapour-diffusion to perform crystallization on IMP-6 and IMP-1, expecting to obtain rod-shaped crystals.

parameters.IMP-6_condition: 0.1 M sodium acetate (pH 4.6), 0.2 M ammonium acetate, 17% PEG 8000
parameters.IMP-1_condition: 0.1 M sodium acetate (pH 5.0), 0.2 M ammonium acetate, 22% PEG 8000

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Rod-shaped crystals grew to 0.1x0.1x0.2 mm in 2 weeks.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Crystals are grown. [Gap] Diffraction data is needed to solve the structure. [Decision] To achieve data collection, chose to use X-ray Diffraction to perform data collection on IMP-6 and IMP-1 crystals, expecting to obtain diffraction images.

[Background] Diffraction images are collected. [Gap] Raw data needs to be processed into reflections. [Decision] To achieve data processing, chose to use XDS to perform processing on diffraction data, expecting to obtain processed reflection data.

references.citation: Kabsch, W. (2010) XDS. Acta Crystallogr. D Biol. Crystallogr. 66, 125–132
references.relevance: Software used for X-ray data processing

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Processed reflection data is available. [Gap] Initial phases are needed to solve the structure. [Decision] To achieve structure solution, chose to use Molrep to perform molecular replacement on reflection data, expecting to obtain initial phases.

references.citation: Vagin, A., and Teplyakov, A. (2010) Molecular replacement with MOLREP. Acta Crystallogr. D Biol. Crystallogr. 66, 22–25
Collaborative Computational Project, Number 4 (1994) The CCP4 suite: programs for protein crystallography. Acta Crystallogr. D Biol. Crystallogr. 50, 760–763

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial phases are obtained. [Gap] The atomic model needs to be built and adjusted. [Decision] To achieve model building, chose to use Coot to perform manual model building on initial structure, expecting to obtain built atomic model.

references.citation: Emsley, P., Lohkamp, B., Scott, W.G., and Cowtan, K. (2010) Features and development of Coot. Acta Crystallogr. D Biol. Crystallogr. 66, 486–501

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Atomic model is built. [Gap] The model needs to be refined against data. [Decision] To achieve structure refinement, chose to use Refmac5 to perform refinement on atomic model, expecting to obtain refined structures with R-factors.

references.citation: Murshudov, G.N., Vagin, A.A., and Dodson, E.J. (1997) Refinement of macromolecular structures by the maximum-likelihood method. Acta Crystallogr. D Biol. Crystallogr. 53, 240–255

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refined structures are available. [Gap] Substrate binding modes need to be modeled. [Decision] To achieve substrate docking, chose to use MF myPresto to perform docking simulation between IMP-6 and hydrolysed substrates, expecting to obtain docking models.

parameters.templates: IMP-13 in complex with hydrolysed imipenem (PDB ID 6RZS) or meropenem (PDB ID 6RZR)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Proposed docking models between IMP-6 and hydrolysed substrates generated.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Wild-type structures and kinetics are characterized. [Gap] The role of Pro68 in loop flexibility needs validation. [Decision] To achieve mutant generation, chose to use KOD plus mutagenesis kit to perform site-directed mutagenesis on IMP-6 and IMP-1, expecting to obtain P68G mutant plasmids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: P68G mutants of IMP-6 and IMP-1 constructed.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] P68G mutant plasmids are constructed. [Gap] Kinetic parameters of mutants need to be measured. [Decision] To achieve kinetic measurement, chose to use Spectrophotometer to perform enzyme activity assays on P68G mutants, expecting to obtain initial steady-state velocities.

parameters.buffer: 20 mM HEPES buffer (pH 7.0) with 50 uM ZnSO4

references.citation: Laraki, N., et al. (1999) Biochemical characterization of the Pseudomonas aeruginosa 101/1477 metallo-beta-lactamase IMP-1 produced by Escherichia coli. Antimicrob. Agents Chemother. 43, 902–906

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial steady-state velocities are measured. [Gap] Kinetic constants (Km, kcat) need to be calculated. [Decision] To achieve kinetic analysis, chose to use Lineweaver-Burk Plot Analysis to perform calculation on velocity data, expecting to obtain Km and kcat values.

parameters.method: Double-reciprocal plot of initial steady-state velocities

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Kinetic parameters Km and kcat obtained for P68G mutants.

references.citation: Hiraoka, M., et al. (1988) Cephalosporinase interactions and antimicrobial activity of BMY-28142, ceftazidime and cefotaxime. J. Antibiot. 41, 86–93

IMP-6_resolution.interpretation: High resolution structure of IMP-6 determined.

IMP-1_resolution.interpretation: High resolution structure of IMP-1 determined.

Km_ratio_imipenem_IMP6_vs_IMP1.interpretation: Km for imipenem is 14-fold higher in IMP-6 compared to IMP-1, indicating reduced affinity.

kcat_Km_ratio_meropenem_vs_imipenem_IMP6.interpretation: kcat/Km for meropenem is 7-fold higher than for imipenem in IMP-6.

Km_increase_IMP6_P68G_imipenem.interpretation: Km for imipenem increased by 8-fold in IMP-6 P68G mutant compared to wild type.

B_factor_ratio_loop_nonloop.interpretation: L1 loop of IMP-6 is more flexible than IMP-1 based on B-factor ratios.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The structural and functional impact of the Ser262Gly mutation was elucidated, showing that increased L1 loop flexibility in IMP-6 (due to Gly262) reduces affinity for imipenem while maintaining affinity for meropenem, confirmed by Km values and crystallographic B-factors.
